title: Diffusion and Osmosis
description: Understand particle movement across concentration gradients.
learningOutcomes: Apply diffusion/osmosis to a biological example.

terms.definition: Net movement from high to low concentration.
Net movement of water through a partially permeable membrane.
Difference in concentration between two areas.

content.title: How particles move
content.content: Particles move randomly, but net movement is down a concentration gradient. In osmosis, only water moves through a partially permeable membrane.

content.title: Predict movement
content.problem: A cell is placed in a concentrated sugar solution.

steps.prompt: Is water concentration inside the cell higher or lower than outside?
steps.hint: Concentrated sugar outside means less water outside.

steps.prompt: What is the net direction of water movement?
steps.hint: Water moves from high to low water concentration.

expectedAnswer: movement of water through a partially permeable membrane
net movement of water from dilute to concentrated

questions.questionText: Why does diffusion eventually slow down?
In one sentence, compare diffusion and osmosis.
expectedAnswer: diffusion is movement of particles, osmosis is movement of water
osmosis is a type of diffusion of water
